name: Can I ask follow-up questions with spoken search, or does each query have to stand alone?
description: Most modern voice assistants support follow-up spoken queries that use context from your last question, like refining results or adding filters. Keep follow-ups

mainEntity.name: Can I ask follow-up questions with spoken search, or does each query have to stand alone?

acceptedAnswer.text:
Spoken search supports natural language, so you can ask follow-ups like “What about vegetarian options?” after a restaurant query.
Many assistants track conversational context for a short window.

**Key factors:**
- Whether your assistant supports multi-turn conversations
- How clearly follow-up questions reference the prior topic
- Time elapsed since the previous query
- Device settings for conversational mode

In practice, ask your main question, listen fully, then make one focused follow-up such as “Sort by highest rating” or “Show only kid-friendly options.”
If the assistant loses context, re-state the core topic out loud instead of relying on it to remember.
acceptedAnswer.description: Most modern voice assistants support follow-up spoken queries that use context from your last question, like refining results or adding filters. Keep follow-ups short and clearly tied to the prior topic, and re-state the subject if context is lost.
